day1_insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: hours since high tide is lowering risk — the timing relative to high tide affects how concentrated bacteria are at the shoreline; wave period is lowering risk — longer-period swell reaches deeper and can resuspend sediment-bound bacteria; and the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low. The last lab sample (10 MPN/100mL) is now 28 days old — beaches are sampled about weekly, so a reading this stale carries little weight, and this estimate leans on current conditions.
